studies.title: Prospective randomized controlled trial to evaluate the clinical effectiveness of the app-based therapeutic training program (ViViRA Neck) for patients with non-specific neck pain.

_verification.reason: The study is about 'ViViRA Neck', which is a different app-based therapeutic program for neck pain, not the 'Vivira' product listed for back pain or osteoarthritis of the spine. The study focuses on a different condition and intervention.

studies.title: Patient survey on the usability of the Digital Health Application ViViRA for exercise therapy of nonspecific and degenerative back pain

_verification.reason: The study is specifically about the digital therapeutic product ViViRA, as evidenced by the repeated mention of the product name in the title and full text, and the focus on evaluating its usability for back pain treatment.

_classification.reason: The study is a survey focused on assessing the usability and user experience of the ViViRA app, using a questionnaire to gather feedback from users. It does not involve randomization or control groups, nor does it collect real-world clinical effectiveness data, making it ineligible as a primary RCT or RWE study.

studies._rejection_reason: The study is a survey focused on assessing the usability and user experience of the ViViRA app, using a questionnaire to gather feedback from users. It does not involve randomization or control groups, nor does it collect real-world clinical effectiveness data, making it ineligible as a primary RCT or RWE study.
